textPlain: One could just use the math fragment of typst, no?https://typerino.com/

What exactly is the distinction between this and mathjax? At first glance it looks like this is a wrapper on top of mathjax, or is it something different?I guess it’s just more “natural” and less latex like.$$\sum_{i=0}^n i^2 = \frac{(n)}{2}$$Vssum_(i=o)^n i^2=((n)/2)
It's too bad MathML never supported ASCII math. The web might be a different place if it did.
This is cool. I could see myself using this for notes.
If you like this, check out typst https://typst.app

Asciidoctor renders AsciiMath to MathML [1], I imagine that it's a fairly common target after MathML (Core) was revived from the dead. While MathML is pretty verbose to write by hand, I think things like AsciiMath and LaTeX are on a different level of abstraction. You can't embed AsciiMath directly in HTML in the same way that you can't embed Markdown directly in HTML.1. https://docs.asciidoctor.org/asciidoctor/latest/stem/asciima...

You can use it with AsciiDoc readily, if you use that [1]. With anything you could also use MathML in an HTML-passthrough block, but it's pretty verbose.1. https://docs.asciidoctor.org/asciidoctor/latest/stem/asciima...
